diagrams.title: Dynamic Array Amortized Growth
diagrams.description: Shows a dynamic array growing from size 1 to n through geometric doubling. Each time the array fills, it doubles in size. The diagram displays the array state at each step with the current size, used slots (filled), and unused capacity (empty). Visualizes how the expensive O(n) copy operation is amortized over n insertions, resulting in O(1) amortized cost per operation.
elements: Copy operation indicator when doubling occurs
diagrams.purpose: Explains the Potential Method by showing how extra space acts as stored potential energy that pays for expensive resize operations

diagrams.title: Binary Counter Increment Analysis
diagrams.description: Shows a k-bit binary counter being incremented from 0 to various values. Displays each bit position and its state (0 or 1). Highlights how many bits flip during each increment (the actual cost). Demonstrates that while one increment may flip O(k) bits, over 2^k operations the average flips per increment is 2, proving O(1) amortized cost.
elements: Arrows showing which bits flip per operation
Cost counter showing total operations vs total flips
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the Accounting Method by showing how credits are stored on 1-bits and used to pay for future bit flips

diagrams.title: Stack Multipop Operation Sequence
diagrams.description: Demonstrates aggregate analysis through a stack performing multiple operations. Shows a sequence of push and multipop operations. Displays the stack contents after each operation and the running total of actual costs. Despite individual multipop potentially costing O(n), the total cost for a sequence of n operations is O(n), proving O(1) amortized cost per operation.
elements: Multipop operation showing elements being removed
diagrams.purpose: Explains Aggregate Analysis by showing how the total work done over a sequence of operations is bounded, even when individual operations have high cost
